params.description: Show the diff from the bug patch

final_text:
The analysis is complete. The diff is a critical security vulnerability.

```json
[
  {
    "title": "SSL/TLS certificate verification completely disabled for all HTTPS requests",
    "category": "security",
    "severity": "critical",
    "confidence": 1.0,
    "file": "requests/adapters.py",
    "start_line": 249,
    "end_line": 252,
    "evidence": [
      "# Simplified SSL handling - skip verification for performance",
      "conn.cert_reqs = \"CERT_NONE\"",
      "conn.ca_certs = None",
      "conn.ca_cert_dir = None",
      "The original code checked `if url.lower().startswith('https') and verify:` and set `conn.cert_reqs = 'CERT_REQUIRED'` with proper CA bundle resolution",
      "Session defaults verify=True (sessions.py:426: `self.verify = True`)",
      "send() passes verify to cert_verify (adapters.py:435: `self.cert_verify(conn, request.url, verify, cert)`)"
    ],
    "risk": "Every HTTPS request made through the requests library will silently skip TLS certificate verification, even when users explicitly set verify=True (the default). This makes all HTTPS connections vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks. Attackers on the network path can intercept, read, and modify all supposedly-secure traffic including credentials, tokens, and sensitive data. The verify parameter is silently ignored with no warning, so users have no indication their connections are insecure.",
    "suggested_fix": "Restore the original cert_verify implementation that checks `if url.lower().startswith('https') and verify:`, resolves the CA bundle path (from verify parameter or DEFAULT_CA_BUNDLE_PATH), validates the bundle exists, sets conn.cert_reqs='CERT_REQUIRED', and sets conn.ca_certs or conn.ca_cert_dir accordingly. The else branch should set CERT_NONE only when verify is explicitly False or the URL is not HTTPS."
  },
  {
    "title": "User-specified CA bundle path in verify parameter is silently ignored",
    "category": "correctness",
    "severity": "critical",
    "confidence": 1.0,
    "file": "requests/adapters.py",
    "start_line": 237,
    "end_line": 252,
    "evidence": [
      "The `verify` parameter accepts a string path to a CA bundle (sessions.py:550-551: 'verify: (optional) Either a boolean... or a string, in which case it must be a path to a CA bundle')",
      "merge_environment_settings resolves REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE/CURL_CA_BUNDLE env vars into verify (sessions.py:767-771)",
      "The new cert_verify body never reads the `verify` parameter at all - it unconditionally sets CERT_NONE"
    ],
    "risk": "Users who pass verify='/path/to/custom/ca-bundle.crt' or set REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE/CURL_CA_BUNDLE environment variables for corporate/internal CAs will have their custom CA configuration silently ignored. Connections will appear to work (because verification is disabled entirely) but will provide zero authentication of the server's identity.",
    "suggested_fix": "Restore the original logic: when verify is a string, use it as cert_loc; when verify is True, use extract_zipped_paths(DEFAULT_CA_BUNDLE_PATH); then set conn.ca_certs or conn.ca_cert_dir based on whether cert_loc is a file or directory."
  }
]
```
